completion_criteria_checked: scripts/generate-dashboard.ps1 is self-contained and strictly read-only over the .cockpit/ bridge: reads only the two state files by parameter, writes only -OutputPath (default dashboard/index.html), creates only the output's parent directory, calls no other script, and contains no code path that writes into .cockpit/.

The reported JSON-null-cast rendering bug and its fix were checked against the current script: every rendered value passes through Encode-Html, which now checks [string]::IsNullOrEmpty after the [string] cast rather than checking for $null before it, correctly producing '(none)' for verification_verdict and current_blocker. No other live field in the script remains exposed to the same blank-cell failure.

The task's own Process Disclosure (worker-result.md) was checked against the mandatory pre-task handoff/backup gate (scripts/enforce-handoff-restart-safety.ps1): confirmed the gate was skipped -- task-state moved directly to in_progress without passing through ready_for_worker -- so no true pre-task backup existed before the task's changes were made. The later backup is retroactive only, not a genuine pre-task restore point.
evidence_reviewed: scripts/generate-dashboard.ps1 (full file, read via remote repo access)
.cockpit/result/worker-result.md (full file, including its Process Disclosure section)
missing_evidence: A true pre-task backup/restore point taken before this task's changes were made (the mandatory gate that would have produced one, scripts/enforce-handoff-restart-safety.ps1, was not run before work started).
risks: The delivered script is technically sound (read-only, self-contained, no engine-script calls, no .cockpit/ writes) and the reported null-cast bug fix is correct and complete for this script's rendering path.

The deficiency is process-compliance, not artifact quality: the mandatory pre-task handoff/backup gate (scripts/enforce-handoff-restart-safety.ps1) was skipped this cycle, so no genuine pre-task restore point exists -- only a retroactive, post-hoc backup taken after the changes were already made.

DECISION-089 as currently recorded does not mention the skipped gate / missing true pre-task backup that worker-result.md discloses; as written, repo truth (the decision log) reads cleaner than the actual cycle was. This should be corrected regardless of how the task itself is resolved.

This verification was performed via the ChatGPT manual bridge (DECISION-086) with remote, read-only repo access only. It has no local execution and did not independently re-run scripts/verify-handback-guardrails.ps1 or other local guardrails; per docs/VERIFICATION_RESULT_SPEC.md, this is additive review, not a substitute for local-guardrail-based independent verification.
summary: The delivered script (scripts/generate-dashboard.ps1) is genuinely read-only, self-contained, and satisfies the extractability contract; the reported null-cast bug and its fix both check out. However, this is not a clean accept cycle: the worker's own Process Disclosure confirms the mandatory pre-task handoff/backup gate was skipped, so no true pre-task restore point exists, only a retroactive one. That is a real missing safeguard, not merely a caveat, so the cycle is INSUFFICIENT rather than PASS. Separately, DECISION-089 should be corrected to name this gap plainly, since as written it does not disclose what worker-result.md already discloses.
next_action: Owner decision required: either (a) explicitly override-accept this cycle given the artifact is sound and the gap is fully disclosed and already partially mitigated by git history preserving the true pre-task state, recording the override plainly in repo truth, or (b) treat this as a genuine process failure requiring the gate to be run properly on a corrective task. Either way, correct DECISION-089 first so it names the skipped gate / missing true pre-task backup rather than omitting it.
